channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Filtered Reasoning Score: Evaluating Reasoning Quality on a Model's Most-Confident Traces
item.description: arXiv:2604.11996v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Should we trust Large Language Models (LLMs) with high accuracy? LLMs achieve high accuracy on reasoning benchmarks, but correctness alone does not reveal the quality of the reasoning used to produce it. This highlights a fundamental limitation of out...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, RAG, large language model, example, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Think Through Uncertainty: Improving Long-Form Generation Factuality via Reasoning Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2604.12046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) often hallucinate in long-form generation. Existing approaches mainly improve factuality through post-hoc revision or reinforcement learning (RL) with correctness-based rewards, but they do not teach the model to estimate ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, RAG, large language model, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Empirical Evaluation of PDF Parsing and Chunking for Financial Question Answering with RAG
item.description: arXiv:2604.12047v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: PDF files are primarily intended for human reading rather than automated processing. In addition, the heterogeneous content of PDFs, such as text, tables, and images, poses significant challenges for parsing and information extraction. To address thes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, study, arxiv, research, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Leveraging Weighted Syntactic and Semantic Context Assessment Summary (wSSAS) Towards Text Categorization Using LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2604.12049v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for reliable, enterprise-grade analytics such as text categorization is often hindered by the stochastic nature of attention mechanisms and sensitivity to noise that compromise their analytical precision and rep...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, LLM, RAG, large language model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GoodPoint: Learning Constructive Scientific Paper Feedback from Author Responses
item.description: arXiv:2604.11924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While LLMs hold significant potential to transform scientific research, we advocate for their use to augment and empower researchers rather than to automate research without human oversight. To this end, we study constructive feedback generation, the ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, RAG, study, arxiv, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Long-Horizon Task Mirage? Diagnosing Where and Why Agentic Systems Break
item.description: arXiv:2604.11978v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents perform strongly on short- and mid-horizon tasks, but often break down on long-horizon tasks that require extended, interdependent action sequences. Despite rapid progress in agentic systems, these long-horizon failur...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, RAG, large language model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When to Forget: A Memory Governance Primitive
item.description: arXiv:2604.12007v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent memory systems accumulate experience but currently lack a principled operational metric for memory quality governance -- deciding which memories to trust, suppress, or deprecate as the agent's task distribution shifts. Write-time importance scor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, embedding, paper, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Identity as Attractor: Geometric Evidence for Persistent Agent Architecture in LLM Activation Space
item.description: arXiv:2604.12016v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models map semantically related prompts to similar internal representations -- a phenomenon interpretable as attractor-like dynamics. We ask whether the identity document of a persistent cognitive agent (its cognitive_core) exhibits ana...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, arxiv, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A longitudinal health agent framework
item.description: arXiv:2604.12019v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although artificial intelligence (AI) agents are increasingly proposed to support potentially longitudinal health tasks, such as symptom management, behavior change, and patient support, most current implementations fall short of facilitating user int...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, framework, arxiv, research, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Layer-wise Analysis of Supervised Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2604.11838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While critical for alignment, Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) incurs the risk of catastrophic forgetting, yet the layer-wise emergence of instruction-following capabilities remains elusive. We investigate this mechanism via a comprehensive analysis utili...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, alignment, arxiv, analysis, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Reasoning Models Hurt Behavioral Simulation: A Solver-Sampler Mismatch in Multi-Agent LLM Negotiation
item.description: arXiv:2604.11840v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly used as agents in social, economic, and policy simulations. A common assumption is that stronger reasoning should improve simulation fidelity. We argue that this assumption can fail when the objective is not to s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, RAG, large language model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Polynomial Expansion Rank Adaptation: Enhancing Low-Rank Fine-Tuning with High-Order Interactions
item.description: arXiv:2604.11841v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-rank adaptation (LoRA) is a widely used strategy for efficient fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs), but its strictly linear structure fundamentally limits expressive capacity. The bilinear formulation of weight updates captures only first-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, arxiv, analysis, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Disposition Distillation at Small Scale: A Three-Arc Negative Result
item.description: arXiv:2604.11867v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We set out to train behavioral dispositions (self-verification, uncertainty acknowledgment, feedback integration) into small language models (0.6B to 2.3B effective parameters) through a four-stage all-MIT distillation pipeline, with follow-on experim...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, model, experiment, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Subcritical Signal Propagation at Initialization in Normalization-Free Transformers
item.description: arXiv:2604.11890v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study signal propagation at initialization in transformers through the averaged partial Jacobian norm (APJN), a measure of gradient amplification across layers. We extend APJN analysis to transformers with bidirectional attention and permutation-sy...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, study, arxiv, analysis, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Transformers Learn to Plan via Multi-Token Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2604.11912v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While next-token prediction (NTP) has been the standard objective for training language models, it often struggles to capture global structure in reasoning tasks. Multi-token prediction (MTP) has recently emerged as a promising alternative, yet its un...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, paper, model, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Self-Monitoring Benefits from Structural Integration: Lessons from Metacognition in Continuous-Time Multi-Timescale Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.11914v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-monitoring capabilities -- metacognition, self-prediction, and subjective duration -- are often proposed as useful additions to reinforcement learning agents. But do they actually help? We investigate this question in a continuous-time multi-time...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Narrative-Driven Paper-to-Slide Generation via ArcDeck
item.description: arXiv:2604.11969v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce ArcDeck, a multi-agent framework that formulates paper-to-slide generation as a structured narrative reconstruction task. Unlike existing methods that directly summarize raw text into slides, ArcDeck explicitly models the source paper's l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, paper, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
